programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Following news of the history-making discovery of Sir Ernest Shackleton's ship the Endurance, this is a look at his historic and ill-fated 1914 Antarctic expedition resulting in the loss of the highly revered ship.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: On Christmas morning in 1945, five siblings vanish from their family home in West Virginia during a raging fire; despite the work of dozens of investigators and thousands of amateur sleuths, the mystery remains.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: They've been called the world's greatest archaeological enigma: 2,000-year-old geoglyphs made in the Peruvian desert that span over 50 miles; the drawings are so massive, they can only be seen from the air.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: In July 1996, one of the worst aviation disasters in American history leaves 230 people dead and countless questions unanswered; it's a tragedy that changes the aviation industry forever.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: A look at whether or not intelligent robots are a threat to humanity or the next step in human evolution; as man and machine begin to merge, humanity could be fulfilling a destiny prepared for by aliens thousands of years ago.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Satellite images have revealed a giant face that appears to be carved into the Earth in Alberta, Canada, which has now become known far and wide as "The Badlands Guardian."

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Despite all of our modern technology, there are aspects of some vanishings that are bizarre and simply can't be explained.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: The world is full of stunning displays of natural phenomena that are so spectacular and, at times, so strange they defy conventional wisdom; examining if science can explain these mysteries.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: For thousands of years, people have believed in the existence of Satan, the embodiment of evil; some believe he has the power to possess souls and create mayhem amongst humanity.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Throughout history and across the globe, there have been many sacred relics believed to be imbued with mystical powers.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Many people are afraid of walking into a cursed place or touching a reportedly cursed object; looking at if whether or not curses are real and how are they brought into existence.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Every year, thousands of people report looking up at the sky and witnessing unidentified flying objects. A vast majority of these strange and disturbing incidents take place in the same locations on Earth. Is it possible that extraterrestrial spacecraft are repeatedly visiting Earth at so-called "UFO Hot Spots?"

programme.title: Escaping Evil: My Life in a Cult
programme.desc: A man blindly follows black supremacist Yahweh ben Yahweh until he suffers at his leader's hands; a military-structured religious community forces a woman to beat her children.

programme.title: Cults and Extreme Belief
programme.desc: Despite their hippie persona and rustic community businesses, the Twelve Tribes have long stood accused of physically abusing children and subjugating of women.

programme.title: Cults and Extreme Belief
programme.desc: Growing up in the United Nation of Islam (UNOI), Elijah Muhammad was forced into child labor, physical abuse and squalid living conditions. Today, he has a unique opportunity to speak to the FBI in hope of getting justice for himself and the other children abused by UNOI and its leader, Royall Jenkins', teachings.

programme.title: Cults and Extreme Belief
programme.desc: Amy Bril recounts her childhood growing up in the Children of God, a group notorious for pedophilia and "religious" prostitution.

programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States tackles the British police and goes on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of the double-murderer who killed a 77-year-old teacher and a 59-year-old reverend.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Edward Johnson was sent to prison for the brutal murder of a female friend, but the violence didn't end there.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: At 18, Christa Pike commits a brutal murder and becomes one of the youngest ever women sent to death row.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: His time behind bars only made Italian-American mobster Freddy Geas even more brutal.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A Texas serial killer, Anthony Allen Shore was matched to four murders after his arrest for a different crime.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look at the man convicted of sexually assaulting and murdering two nine-year-old girls in 1986.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A double murderer is found guilty of the brutal assassination of an elderly couple.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, FBI agents began investigating New York gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. They soon realized that Dhinsa was more than a crooked businessman, he enforced his mob-like operation with brutal violence, kidnapping and killing errant employees and any family members who questioned their deaths. FBI agents worked their way into Dhinsa's violent world.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When five-year-old Melissa Brannen disappeared from a Christmas party in December 1989, the only probable suspect was 24-year-old Caleb Hughes. Hughes reportedly left the party around the time Melissa vanished, but no one saw him with the girl. Fifty blue acrylic fibers collected during a search of Hughes' car, however, became the major piece of evidence in the case. FBI fiber analysis determined that those fibers were identical to the ones that made up the sweater Melissa was wearing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1980, the FBI began a massive undercover operation, code named Safebet, that was designed to expose the Chicago Mafia's infiltration into the city's surrounding suburbs. Undercover agents quickly discovered that the mob had its tentacles spread into every facet of the law enforcement community. They unwittingly uncovered the murder of a wealthy socialite perpetrated by a jealous and greedy mob lawyer and covered up by the local police chief.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In November of 1994, when a Wells Fargo armored van disappeared from a Glendale, Arizona mall, local authorities and the FBI scrambled to recover it, and its cargo of nearly a million dollars. Six hours later, agents found the van, its driver shot dead inside and the money gone. It was a perfect hit by professionals who left no clue behind. Months passed before a solid lead developed. In January, a tip led agents to look into a former prison guard.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished into the high desert, along with the two men hired to guard it. The FBI didn't know if the guards were participants or victims of an ambush. Answers lay somewhere in the thousands of square miles of Arizona's northwest territory. When the vehicle was recovered with three hundred thousand dollars and the drivers missing, suspicions that the crime was an inside job grew.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Langley, Virginia, a gunman fired into rush hour traffic. The brazen daytime shooting of employees from the Central Intelligence Agency stunned the country. The victims seemed randomly selected, but the motive appeared to be political. The FBI was called in, but the nationwide search quickly became a far-reaching international manhunt. Agents processed more than 2000 leads and crossed an ocean in the effort to deliver justice.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a Delaware woman vanished in 1996, family and friends discovered her secret life. The appointments secretary to the Governor had carried on an illicit affair with a wealthy married man. Agents suspected that her powerful ex-lover was responsible, but they had little physical evidence to support their theory. Investigators never found the woman's body or the murder weapon. The FBI's only hope was to go undercover, and turn brother against brother.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, FBI agents began investigating New York gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. They soon realized that Dhinsa was more than a crooked businessman, he enforced his mob-like operation with brutal violence, kidnapping and killing errant employees and any family members who questioned their deaths. FBI agents worked their way into Dhinsa's violent world.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1980s, the decapitation of a young African-American male led the Miami-Dade police department and the FBI along a trail of murder. Investigators quickly discovered that the victim was a dissident member of a religious sect headed by a man calling himself Yahweh ben Yahweh, or God, Son of God. Although the FBI believed Yahweh ben Yahweh and his followers were responsible for many murders, they could find no direct evidence linking them to the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: By April of 1995 in Washington, D.C., two officers had been shot and one had been murdered. Each had been sitting in their patrol cars when a man sneaked up and opened fire. The FBI was brought in to help catch the cop killer. Agents identified the man who was most likely the killer, but he was nowhere to be found. Months of surveillance operations around the country finally yielded a break; they found their suspect in Baltimore, MD.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A 1981 armored car robbery near New York City left one guard dead and another critically wounded. Police stopped suspects nearby and another shoot-out ensued, claiming the lives of two officers. Local police and the FBI suspected a large group of domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. They would try to ferret out these heavily armed criminals who moved in a shadowy underworld and were not afraid to kill authorities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1990's, a gang of professional bank robbers raided cities across seven states. Disguises and speed protected their identity from cameras and the police as they stole hundreds of thousands of dollars, always leaving a bomb to terrorize their victims. The stakes were raised when the FBI discovered the robbers had an agenda beyond their personal wealth. To stop these extremists and their growing operation, the FBI needed to capture their leaders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Corruption in the New Orleans Police Department grew like a cancer in the early 1990's. Lured by the easy wealth of the narcotics industry, crooked cops began breaking the laws they were sworn to uphold. They would stop at nothing, including murder, to protect their rackets. When it became clear they could no longer police themselves, the FBI got involved. Putting their lives on the line, undercover agents devised a way to flush out the violent cops and restore trust in the city's police force.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished into the high desert, along with the two men hired to guard it. The FBI didn't know if the guards were participants or victims of an ambush. Answers lay somewhere in the thousands of square miles of Arizona's northwest territory. When the vehicle was recovered with three hundred thousand dollars and the drivers missing, suspicions that the crime was an inside job grew.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984 had the authorities puzzled and the community scared. Since the beginning, the FBI had been analyzing the crime scene evidence and had found a common link: red trilobal carpet fibers. But there was no suspect, until one of the killer's potential victims survived. Through her interviews and the cooperation of local authorities and the FBI, 31-year-old Bobby Joe Long emerged as the prime suspect.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a series of brutal bank robberies and murders in south Miami during the mid-1980's, the FBI embarked on an exhaustive eight-month investigation. With little evidence and no suspects, agents were willing to try anything to find the violent perpetrators. On April 11, 1986, agents conducting routine surveillance spotted the vehicle used in the bank robberies. They attempted to arrest the two suspects in the car. What followed was the bloodiest firefight in FBI history.

programme.title: Los Archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El primero de octubre de 1993, un extraño secuestró a Polly Klaas de doce años durante una piyamada en su casa. El FBI de inmediato entró en el caso. Los agentes reunieron fibras de su habitación y descubrieron, a través del uso de fuentes de luz láser, la huella de una palma en la cama de Polly, previamente invisible al ojo humano. Después de una extensa investigación de dos meses, el FBI arrestó a Richard Allen Davis. La impresión de la palma y las fibras coincidieron y Davis fue condenado por secuestro y asesinato.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, south Miami became the home for a sadistic serial killer. Posing as a fashion photographer, Christopher Wilder lured young women from shopping malls with promises of fame and fortune. Most ended up dead. As the FBI closed in around him, Wilder took off on a cross-country murderous rampage. The FBI added Wilder to their Most Wanted Fugitives program. The chase was on, and Wilder ultimately lost.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In rural Texas, 13-year-old is kidnapped on her way to school. Frantic to find her safely, her millionaire father calls local authorities and the FBI. As police officers and agents conduct aerial surveillance, the father drives hundreds of miles through the night to deliver the ransom cash to a drop that the kidnappers keep changing. In a final effort to save the young girl, investigators must engage in a dangerous firefight with five desperate criminals.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a teenage immigrant came to the United States to realize her dream of becoming a doctor. But a tragic twist of fate intervened, when she found herself an innocent victim of the drug trade. Kidnapped and held captive, she survived for days against all odds. Authorities arrested her captors, but they refused to reveal her whereabouts. The FBI and local police raced against time, determined to save the teenager's life. Their investigation revealed a complex story of drugs, betrayal and revenge.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer night in 1990, a body was discovered burning in a Minnesota alley. Weeks later, police finally identified the victim, a suspected drug courier from California. The FBI began working with local police and slowly uncovered a multi-state crime ring run by a deadly drug lord who used violence and intimidation to keep those around him silent. Agents needed to infiltrate and dismantle the crew before more people died.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, a triple murder terrified residents in the nation's capital. In a Georgetown coffee shop, three employees were shot to death. Investigators could find no immediate suspects and no clear motive for the attack. Evidence at the crime scene pointed in many different directions. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong, or an act of cold-blooded vengeance against one of the victims?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: He wandered the streets of New York City wearing only pajamas and a bathrobe, but the FBI was convinced that Vince Gigante, the reputed boss of the Genovese crime family, was anything but crazy. The FBI used undercover surveillance, secret audio tapes, six mobsters turned government informants and the testimony of twelve FBI agents to help convict Gigante of murder and conspiracy-to-murder, including a plot to kill rival mob boss John Gotti.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a chilly afternoon in 1989, Marine Captain Shirley Russell disappeared from Quantico, Virginia. Captain Russell was a dedicated officer; her friends and colleagues were certain she had not gone AWOL. FBI agents found her husband, a former Marine, was acting strangely, and a blood-like stain on the couple's floor disappeared before agents could examine it. There was no body, no witness and no weapon.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When five-year-old Melissa Brannen disappeared from a Christmas party in December 1989, the only probable suspect was 24-year-old Caleb Hughes. Hughes reportedly left the party around the time Melissa vanished, but no one saw him with the girl. Fifty blue acrylic fibers collected during a search of Hughes' car, however, became the major piece of evidence in the case. FBI fiber analysis determined that those fibers were identical to the ones that made up the sweater Melissa was wearing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1988, a wealthy Jackson, Mississippi woman disappeared from her home in broad daylight. Left behind was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names and no specific instructions. The note, and a few drops of blood were the only clues to her fate. FBI and local police struggled to find answers in the cryptic message so they could find the kidnapper and retrieve the victim before her time ran out. Solving the crime required the keen insight of a criminal profiler and the perseverance of a family, local authorities, and the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: At first it seemed like a tragic hunting accident when 18-year-old David Wilkey was shot to death in rural Virginia in 1983. A tenacious insurance investigator suspected foul play, but he had no direct evidence. Two years later a man contacted North Carolina FBI agents with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire: he claimed he had been hired to murder Wilkey's killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two couples found themselves on the same deserted island paradise. But only one couple left alive. Buck Walker and Stephanie Stearns arrived in Hawaii on the Sea Wind, which belonged to the missing couple, Mac and Muff Graham. Buck and Stephanie were arrested and served time for boat theft. But years later, bones were discovered on the deserted island and identified as the remains of Muff Graham.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer night in 1990, a body was discovered burning in a Minnesota alley. Weeks later, police finally identified the victim, a suspected drug courier from California. The FBI began working with local police and slowly uncovered a multi-state crime ring run by a deadly drug lord who used violence and intimidation to keep those around him silent. Agents needed to infiltrate and dismantle the crew before more people died.

programme.title: Los Archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El 21 de junio de 1964, tres defensores de los derechos civiles, uno blanco y uno negro, desaparecieron de Mississippi mientras cruzaban el condado de Neshoba. Una investigación del FBI a gran escala llevó a la recuperación de los cuerpos de los hombres asesinados y la evidencia física fue recuperada y analizada. Los laboratorios del FBI confirmaron que todas excepto una de las balas usadas para asesinar a los hombres provenían de la misma arma. El FBI se ganó la confianza de un informante, cuya información condujo a la condena de siete miembros del Ku Klux Klan local.

programme.desc: In the mid-1990's, a gang of professional bank robbers raided cities across seven states. programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On the day before Thanksgiving, a mother discovered her 10 year-old son was missing from his school. No one knew how it could have happened. Within hours, the boy's entire family was frantically searching for him. The few leads in the case quickly turned cold and the FBI was called in to aid in the investigation. The search by agents and local authorities would cover a thousand miles to expose the kidnapper and find the missing child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, FBI agents began investigating New York gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. FBI agents worked their way into Dhinsa's violent world.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On February 26, 1993, an explosion rocked the World Trade Center parking garage. Six people died and more than a thousand were injured in the bomb blast that tore a 5-story crater in the building. During the investigation, the twisted fender of a rental van was extracted from the rubble. The FBI was able to determine from where the van was rented and by whom. Undercover agents then made their arrests within six days of the bombing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: By December 1985, John Gotti had assumed his place as the new Don of the Gambino crime family in New York with the assassination of his boss, Paul Castellano. The Teflon Don felt secure in his position, always remaining one step ahead of the law. But he was also the ongoing obsession of Bruce Mouw, leader of an elite crew of FBI agents formed to bring down the Gambino family. With the help and genius of his Special Projects Squad, bugs were planted in Gotti's most private meeting places.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a judge and his politician wife were brutally murdered in their home. The killer left few clues and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI was patiently waiting in the wings. Once it was determined that federal laws had been violated, they pounced on the investigation. They uncovered a corrupt local government and a group of violent criminal conspirators known as the Dixie Mafia, responsible for fraud, money laundering and murder.

programme.title: Ukrainian Sniper
programme.desc: A Ukrainian sniper navigates a warzone and a pregnancy, carving out a place for more women on the frontlines.

programme.title: Blocking The Sun
programme.desc: Wildfires and heat waves are pushing governments to consider a controversial question: Could we artificially reflect the sun from our atmosphere and cool our planet's rising temperature? And more importantly, should we? "In Real Life" rides along in the world's largest flying laboratory and sees the dire stakes surrounding a drastic step to curb climate change.

programme.title: The Keyes To Murder
programme.desc: A self-taught serial killer crisscrosses the country at random, targeting victims of opportunity and murdering them with pre-stashed weapons and tools he calls "kill kits." Law enforcement is clueless to the madman's actions until he breaks his own rule and targets a victim close to home.

programme.title: If The Walls Could Talk
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance and presumed death of a surgeon's wife sparks a feverish homicide investigation, which quickly goes cold. It takes law enforcement 15 years before they can gather enough circumstantial evidence to bring her killer to justice, even though they never find her body.

programme.title: Homicide In The High Desert
programme.desc: When the soon-to-be ex-wife of a prominent investment banker dies inside her Arizona home, it initially appears as though she suffered an accidental fall. Yet, through the dogged persistence of family and friends, and after months of intense investigation, law enforcement discovers the murder was no accident, but an act of rage carried out by a desperate man with hundreds of thousands to gain.

programme.title: Bonded In Murder
programme.desc: A street preacher and his teenage protege run an Internet scam to rob unsuspecting men, steal their identities, then dump their dead bodies in the woods. The scheme works multiple times until one of the marks escapes and brings law enforcement right to their doorstep.

programme.title: Of Vengeance & Murder
programme.desc: A destitute criminal terrorizes central Florida, robbing middles aged men, executing them and disposing of their dead bodies in random wooded areas. The homicides continue for two years until a bloody handprint leads to the capture of a serial killer who happens to be a woman.

programme.title: The Honeybee Killer
programme.desc: A mentally unstable man goes on a two-hour shooting spree, killing one man and wounding two others. Cops have no suspects in that homicide, until the shooter resurfaces to rob a tanning salon and ends up face-to-face with a iron-willed customer.

programme.title: The Bad Son
programme.desc: A gunman attempts to take out an entire family one by one as they enter their house. It takes law enforcement nearly two years to crack the code behind the murders only to find out they were masterminded by someone too close for comfort.

programme.title: Killer Rabbi
programme.desc: When a rabbi's wife is savagely murdered inside the family's suburban New Jersey home, it looks like a robbery gone wrong. After years of chasing down leads, police catch a break, in the form of a co-conspirator whose confession shines a spotlight on a sadistic murder plot.

programme.title: Judgment Of The Hammer Murder With Ashleigh Banfield
programme.desc: Mark Sievers thought he had arranged the perfect murder when he hired a childhood friend and another man to murder his wife, Dr. Teresa Sievers. His best laid plan started falling apart when someone he trusted the most turned on him.

programme.title: Injected
programme.desc: "In Real Life" investigates police and paramedics' use of a controversial chemical used as a sedative on suspects, sometimes with deadly results. Investigative correspondent Lori Jane Gliha guides viewers through a years-long reporting effort to unveil the truth behind ketamine use in emergency situations.

programme.title: Mezcal Gold Rush
programme.desc: A look inside the gold rush that is the mezcal market and the chaos it creates at the popular liquor's source.

programme.title: Today As It Happened
programme.desc: What happened today? Find out as Scripps News gets you up to speed on the day's events that everyone is talking about.

programme.title: Scripps News Showcase: When We Were Apollo
programme.desc: A profile of the engineers, technicians, builders and contractors behind the Apollo program.

programme.title: Sex, Lies and Deepfakes
programme.desc: Online deepfakes and disinformation are targeting women, including prom photos turned into porn. Special explores the devastating impact on women in the age of online harassment.

programme.title: The Moses Diaries
programme.desc: 'Moses' captures the reality of war through his own camera, offering a raw, unfiltered perspective on the sacrifices of a Ukrainian generation fighting for freedom and a window into the grim realities of the frontline that Ukraine commanders do not want the world to see.

programme.title: Victim to Verdict: Movie Popcorn Murder
programme.desc: A Sunday afternoon movie date turns deadly when a confrontation over a cell phone and a bag of popcorn ends with a gunshot. Retired police captain Curtis Reeves claims he acted in self-defense, but will a jury believe that his life was in danger?

programme.title: Asia's Coolest Creatures
programme.desc: This episode features some of the coolest creatures from the continent of Asia. Find out how the Snow Leopard is able to survive in the Himalayan Mountains. And why the Komodo Dragon is the largest lizard on earth.

programme.title: Animals of the Land and Sea
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out why the Seahorse swims like an underwater hummingbird. And how the Thomson's Gazelle is able to outrun a Cheetah.

programme.title: Awesome Iceland
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores the famous waterfalls and geysers of Iceland. Then he travels up a volcanic mountain, which is expected to erupt at any moment. Philippe also discovers how Iceland uses geothermal and hydro power to produce all their electricity. Finally, Philippe hikes on one of the largest glaciers in the country.

programme.title: Colors of the Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau travels to Sedona, Arizona, where we look at the spectacular red rock formations. Then we travel to Hawaii, which has red, black and green beaches. We then go to Yellowstone National Park, where some of their hot springs produce brilliant colors due to heat-loving micro-organisms. Finally, we take a look at some colorful animals, including the octopus, which can change its color in an instant.

programme.title: Extreme Animals
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how sharks are able to sleep while they swim. And how the Arctic Fox survives in temperatures that can drop to 70 below.

programme.title: How Animals Survive
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how the peregrine falcon can fly at speeds over 200 miles per hour. And how the alpaca is able to survive at altitudes above 15 thousand feet.

programme.title: The Moses Diaries
programme.desc: 'Moses' captures the reality of war through his own camera, offering a raw, unfiltered perspective on the sacrifices of a Ukrainian generation fighting for freedom and a window into the grim realities of the frontline that Ukraine commanders do not want the world to see.

programme.title: Don't Waste Your Money: Saving Money In 2025
programme.desc: The Don't Waste Your Money team helps you save money on everything from your car to your grocery bills with new tips to trim your budget in 2025.

programme.title: Chibok Kidnapping
programme.desc: On April 14, 2014, 276 mostly Christian female teenage students were kidnapped by the Islamic terrorist group Boko Haram from the Government Girls Secondary School at the town of Chibok in Borno State, Nigeria. This episode features a survivor who escaped the kidnappers and is now a social worker living in Charleston, South Carolina. Dozens of girls are still missing today. The event got international media coverage and attention from a massive social media campaign.

programme.title: Climate Cowboys And Ekso Suits
programme.desc: ScrippsTech travels to the cutting edge, to meet the men and women confronting the world's most vexing problems, with high tech and compelling solutions, medical breakthroughs and innovative discoveries.

programme.title: The Inside Story
programme.desc: In collaboration with "The Marshall Project." In-depth stories on criminal justice - including health care in prisons and jails, staff shortages and controversial forensic evidence.

programme.title: Of Vengeance & Murder
programme.desc: A destitute criminal terrorizes central Florida, robbing middles aged men, executing them and disposing of their dead bodies in random wooded areas. The homicides continue for two years until a bloody handprint leads to the capture of a serial killer who happens to be a woman.

programme.title: The Honeybee Killer
programme.desc: A mentally unstable man goes on a two-hour shooting spree, killing one man and wounding two others. Cops have no suspects in that homicide, until the shooter resurfaces to rob a tanning salon and ends up face-to-face with a iron-willed customer.

programme.title: The Bad Son
programme.desc: A gunman attempts to take out an entire family one by one as they enter their house. It takes law enforcement nearly two years to crack the code behind the murders only to find out they were masterminded by someone too close for comfort.

programme.title: Killer Rabbi
programme.desc: When a rabbi's wife is savagely murdered inside the family's suburban New Jersey home, it looks like a robbery gone wrong. After years of chasing down leads, police catch a break, in the form of a co-conspirator whose confession shines a spotlight on a sadistic murder plot.

programme.title: News Fallback: Hour Long Docs
programme.desc: Long form storytelling from Very Local.

programme.title: Very Local: Very New Mexico
programme.desc: Meet the hosts of the shows on Very Local, and learn about the programs highlighting The Land of Enchantment.

programme.title: Bourbon Blaze: The Night That Tested America's Spirit
programme.desc: When a massive fire hits one of the nation’s largest bourbon distilleries, the families of competing bourbon companies have to decide whether to lend a hand… or let it burn. This is the story of bourbon, America’s native spirit.

programme.title: My Amazing Cheap Date
programme.desc: Two couples sample bites from around the world, cozy up in nature, get hopped up on caffeine and seek out a yoga glow on two blind dates in Orlando.

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: Rob tests out all types of relaxation, from sound baths to flinging an axe, discovers exciting new cuisine and quenches his thirst in this diverse and hopping Boston ‘hood.

programme.title: West By Orphan Train
programme.desc: A gripping documentary uncovering real stories of children loaded on trains, sent west, and presented “for the picking.” From 1854 to 1929, over 6,000 children landed in Iowa communities. Discover their emotional journeys and impact on the Midwest.

programme.title: In Transit by Esquire
programme.desc: Join Esquire’s Dave Holmes as he chats with award-winning musician, Dermot Kennedy, and toasts all things Cincinnati.

programme.title: Outcry: The Search For Answers
programme.desc: A crisis impacting indigenous communities in New Mexico and across the country; the families behind unsolved missing and murdered cases, and the groups hoping to bring answers to their communities.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A teenager vanishes from her home one fall morning in 1999. Her body is discovered 17 days later, seven miles away. It takes more than 15 years for the heartbreaking truth to come out.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Detectives are baffled after a mother and daughter in an affluent family are murdered outside Louisville. A year later, a triple homicide in North Carolina sends detectives on an explosive search, linking the two cases through an unlikely suspect.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Charm City becomes Carb City in this bread-driven battle. Watch as chefs Ferhat Yalcin and Jonathan Hicks see who can carbo-load their way to victory.

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: Printmaking is showcased in Greenville as three young artists vie to take the creative crown. Artistry is put to the test as these makers carve, color and print over two days and four hours to create art-worthy new heights.

programme.title: On The Job
programme.desc: Brendan explores a beautiful orange grove while giving a tour on a school bus monster truck, pampers some pals at a European beer spa, and works the late-shift to make the best Philly cheesesteak outside the City of Brotherly Love.

programme.title: Hit Me With Your Best Spot
programme.desc: Our KC experts include a Savage X Fenty model who also walks his own line, an MTV star who's turning carnivores on to the #plantlife and a local blogger with the scoop on where to let your dog off-leash while enjoying an adult beverage.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Glenda Macatangay has conquered her career and being a single mom to four kids. But when it comes to being comfortable in the outdoors, she isn’t as confident. Kinga meets up with Glenda in Sacramento, CA to introduce her to the wonders of the wilderness.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Seafood starter, seafood main, seafood…dessert? Chef Janine Villalobos and Chef Aziz Bellarbi-Sala push the limits of fish-forward dining to try to ""catch"" the judges’ favor.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Jessy Melo of Jessy’s Clean Meals is back for redemption. Can Chef Nikki Irving, owner of Los Jefes Comida in Taunton, show how she too, can elevate clean and fresh cooking to a higher level or will the blindfold be a game changer?

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: From paying ransoms to scouring hand-drawn treasure maps, these theft victims will do anything to recover their precious property.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Stitch showcases the feel-good and do-good that make up the fabric of America from inspiring tales of perseverance, to good Samaritans helping neighbors, to trailblazers improving their communities.

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: Join host Rob Mariano, also known as Boston Rob from the Survivor series, as he explores the best of Beantown from classic institutions to new local gems.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Comedians, podcasters and craft beer enthusiasts Ed Bailey and Day Bracey explore hot spots, hidden gems and the best places to grab a cold brew in the 'Burgh.

programme.title: Very Local: Very New Mexico
programme.desc: Meet the hosts of the shows on Very Local, and learn about the programs highlighting The Land of Enchantment.

programme.title: Show Me Milwaukee
programme.desc: Local radio personality, Greg Adams, wants to shine a light on all parts of his beloved city and prove that Milwaukee has a lot more to offer than people might expect. In each episode, Greg is joined by a notable personality from the community who shows him their favorite spots and the best ways to experience them.

programme.title: What's On The Menu?
programme.desc: What's On The Menu?

programme.title: Ballin' in Baltimore
programme.desc: Join WNBA player and Olympian Angel McCoughtry as she shows us the people and places that put Baltimore on the map. From sailing the Chesapeake, to sampling street foods, McCoughtry pulls back the curtain on what makes the Charm City so special.

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: Join host Rob Mariano, also known as Boston Rob from the Survivor series, as he explores the best of Beantown from classic institutions to new local gems.

programme.title: KOAT Celebrates 70 Years
programme.desc: For 70 years KOAT has been part of your family. You’ve let us into your homes, to bring you the news that matters most. In this special program, we bring back those familiar faces to revisit some of the biggest stories we have covered over 7 decades.

programme.title: Game Gone Wild: The Socker Slam Story
programme.desc: A stranger than fiction documentary that chronicles a semi-scripted extreme sporting league that premiered in 2000. Socker Slam took elements of wrestling, rugby and “butts up” shenanigans and body slammed them into a soccer drama.

programme.title: KOAT Local News
programme.desc: Get the latest local news and weather from KOAT.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Experience serious vacation vibes and discover some of the most special corners of America on this guided tour of must-visit cities.

programme.title: My Perfect Day
programme.desc: Kar is a selfless soul known for his unwavering commitment to the unhoused in his community. It's Kar's turn to have some good old-fashioned fun.

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: In this game show, comedian Frank Nicotero hits the streets to ask people questions inspired by the wildest news stories for a chance at winning a cash prize. These real-life stories with unreal outcomes will make you say, “Wait, What Happened?"

programme.title: Absolutely Albuquerque
programme.desc: Amanda Machon meets with a different guest each episode who will show off their favorite spots in Albuquerque and the best way to experience this one-of-a-kind city. This series is all about discovering everything that makes ABQ unique and exciting.

programme.title: The Roller Coaster Wars That Birthed a Beast
programme.desc: In 2006, two dozen roller coaster riders landed in the hospital after a freak malfunction in Ohio. This is the story of Kings Island: how its thrilling – and sometimes nail-biting – history spawned one of America’s greatest amusement parks.

programme.title: Ballin' in Baltimore
programme.desc: Join WNBA player and Olympian Angel McCoughtry as she shows us the people and places that put Baltimore on the map. From sailing the Chesapeake, to sampling street foods, McCoughtry pulls back the curtain on what makes the Charm City so special.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Stitch showcases the feel-good and do-good that make up the fabric of America from inspiring tales of perseverance, to good Samaritans helping neighbors, to trailblazers improving their communities.

programme.title: West By Orphan Train
programme.desc: A gripping documentary uncovering real stories of children loaded on trains, sent west, and presented “for the picking.” From 1854 to 1929, over 6,000 children landed in Iowa communities. Discover their emotional journeys and impact on the Midwest.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A true crime series telling the story of unfathomable crimes that have rocked American communities leaving them forever changed.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chefs recreate each other’s signature dish, but they can only determine what the dish is using their senses of taste, touch and smell. When the masks come off, the heat is on, with each chef attempting to cook their opponent’s creation.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Watch things heat up in the kitchen as two local chefs go head-to-head to create a three-course meal utilizing ingredients sourced from one of the city’s unique marketplaces.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: Crimes, Cons & Capers explores some of the wildest headline-grabbing crimes and far-fetched escapades from across the country including bizarre love scams, high-speed car chases, risky prison breaks and more.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Ted wants to live to 100 with his beloved wife beside him. But his oral health threatens his future. Kristy’s a FIXT staffer desperate to smile big on her wedding day. Will love help them face their fears?

programme.title: Judgment Of The Hammer Murder With Ashleigh Banfield
programme.desc: Mark Sievers thought he had arranged the perfect murder when he hired a childhood friend and another man to murder his wife, Dr. Teresa Sievers. His best laid plan started falling apart when someone he trusted the most turned on him.

programme.desc: A look inside the gold rush that is the mezcal market and the chaos it creates at the popular liquor's source.

programme.title: Asia's Coolest Creatures
programme.desc: This episode features some of the coolest creatures from the continent of Asia. Find out how the Snow Leopard is able to survive in the Himalayan Mountains. And why the Komodo Dragon is the largest lizard on earth.

programme.title: Animals of the Land and Sea
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out why the Seahorse swims like an underwater hummingbird. And how the Thomson's Gazelle is able to outrun a Cheetah.

programme.title: Awesome Iceland
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores the famous waterfalls and geysers of Iceland. Then he travels up a volcanic mountain, which is expected to erupt at any moment. Philippe also discovers how Iceland uses geothermal and hydro power to produce all their electricity. Finally, Philippe hikes on one of the largest glaciers in the country.

programme.title: Colors of the Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau travels to Sedona, Arizona, where we look at the spectacular red rock formations. Then we travel to Hawaii, which has red, black and green beaches. We then go to Yellowstone National Park, where some of their hot springs produce brilliant colors due to heat-loving micro-organisms. Finally, we take a look at some colorful animals, including the octopus, which can change its color in an instant.

programme.title: Extreme Animals
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how sharks are able to sleep while they swim. And how the Arctic Fox survives in temperatures that can drop to 70 below.

programme.title: How Animals Survive
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how the peregrine falcon can fly at speeds over 200 miles per hour. And how the alpaca is able to survive at altitudes above 15 thousand feet.

programme.title: The Moses Diaries
programme.desc: 'Moses' captures the reality of war through his own camera, offering a raw, unfiltered perspective on the sacrifices of a Ukrainian generation fighting for freedom and a window into the grim realities of the frontline that Ukraine commanders do not want the world to see.

programme.title: Don't Waste Your Money: Saving Money In 2025
programme.desc: The Don't Waste Your Money team helps you save money on everything from your car to your grocery bills with new tips to trim your budget in 2025.

programme.title: Chibok Kidnapping
programme.desc: On April 14, 2014, 276 mostly Christian female teenage students were kidnapped by the Islamic terrorist group Boko Haram from the Government Girls Secondary School at the town of Chibok in Borno State, Nigeria. This episode features a survivor who escaped the kidnappers and is now a social worker living in Charleston, South Carolina. Dozens of girls are still missing today. The event got international media coverage and attention from a massive social media campaign.

programme.title: Climate Cowboys And Ekso Suits
programme.desc: ScrippsTech travels to the cutting edge, to meet the men and women confronting the world's most vexing problems, with high tech and compelling solutions, medical breakthroughs and innovative discoveries.

programme.title: The Inside Story
programme.desc: In collaboration with "The Marshall Project." In-depth stories on criminal justice - including health care in prisons and jails, staff shortages and controversial forensic evidence.

programme.title: The Inside Story
programme.desc: In collaboration with "The Marshall Project." In-depth stories on criminal justice - including health care in prisons and jails, staff shortages and controversial forensic evidence.

programme.title: Ukrainian Sniper
programme.desc: A Ukrainian sniper navigates a warzone and a pregnancy, carving out a place for more women on the frontlines.

programme.title: Blocking The Sun
programme.desc: Wildfires and heat waves are pushing governments to consider a controversial question: Could we artificially reflect the sun from our atmosphere and cool our planet's rising temperature? And more importantly, should we? "In Real Life" rides along in the world's largest flying laboratory and sees the dire stakes surrounding a drastic step to curb climate change.

programme.title: The Keyes To Murder
programme.desc: A self-taught serial killer crisscrosses the country at random, targeting victims of opportunity and murdering them with pre-stashed weapons and tools he calls "kill kits." Law enforcement is clueless to the madman's actions until he breaks his own rule and targets a victim close to home.

programme.title: If The Walls Could Talk
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance and presumed death of a surgeon's wife sparks a feverish homicide investigation, which quickly goes cold. It takes law enforcement 15 years before they can gather enough circumstantial evidence to bring her killer to justice, even though they never find her body.

programme.title: Homicide In The High Desert
programme.desc: When the soon-to-be ex-wife of a prominent investment banker dies inside her Arizona home, it initially appears as though she suffered an accidental fall. Yet, through the dogged persistence of family and friends, and after months of intense investigation, law enforcement discovers the murder was no accident, but an act of rage carried out by a desperate man with hundreds of thousands to gain.

programme.title: Bonded In Murder
programme.desc: A street preacher and his teenage protege run an Internet scam to rob unsuspecting men, steal their identities, then dump their dead bodies in the woods. The scheme works multiple times until one of the marks escapes and brings law enforcement right to their doorstep.

programme.title: Asia's Coolest Creatures
programme.desc: This episode features some of the coolest creatures from the continent of Asia. Find out how the Snow Leopard is able to survive in the Himalayan Mountains. And why the Komodo Dragon is the largest lizard on earth.

programme.title: Animals of the Land and Sea
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out why the Seahorse swims like an underwater hummingbird. And how the Thomson's Gazelle is able to outrun a Cheetah.

programme.title: Awesome Iceland
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores the famous waterfalls and geysers of Iceland. Then he travels up a volcanic mountain, which is expected to erupt at any moment. Philippe also discovers how Iceland uses geothermal and hydro power to produce all their electricity. Finally, Philippe hikes on one of the largest glaciers in the country.

programme.title: Colors of the Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau travels to Sedona, Arizona, where we look at the spectacular red rock formations. Then we travel to Hawaii, which has red, black and green beaches. We then go to Yellowstone National Park, where some of their hot springs produce brilliant colors due to heat-loving micro-organisms. Finally, we take a look at some colorful animals, including the octopus, which can change its color in an instant.

programme.title: Extreme Animals
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how sharks are able to sleep while they swim. And how the Arctic Fox survives in temperatures that can drop to 70 below.

programme.title: How Animals Survive
programme.desc: This episode features a variety of animals found on land, and in the sea. Find out how the peregrine falcon can fly at speeds over 200 miles per hour. And how the alpaca is able to survive at altitudes above 15 thousand feet.

programme.title: The Moses Diaries
programme.desc: 'Moses' captures the reality of war through his own camera, offering a raw, unfiltered perspective on the sacrifices of a Ukrainian generation fighting for freedom and a window into the grim realities of the frontline that Ukraine commanders do not want the world to see.

programme.title: Don't Waste Your Money: Saving Money In 2025
programme.desc: The Don't Waste Your Money team helps you save money on everything from your car to your grocery bills with new tips to trim your budget in 2025.

programme.title: Chibok Kidnapping
programme.desc: On April 14, 2014, 276 mostly Christian female teenage students were kidnapped by the Islamic terrorist group Boko Haram from the Government Girls Secondary School at the town of Chibok in Borno State, Nigeria. This episode features a survivor who escaped the kidnappers and is now a social worker living in Charleston, South Carolina. Dozens of girls are still missing today. The event got international media coverage and attention from a massive social media campaign.

programme.title: Climate Cowboys And Ekso Suits
programme.desc: ScrippsTech travels to the cutting edge, to meet the men and women confronting the world's most vexing problems, with high tech and compelling solutions, medical breakthroughs and innovative discoveries.

programme.title: The Inside Story
programme.desc: In collaboration with "The Marshall Project." In-depth stories on criminal justice - including health care in prisons and jails, staff shortages and controversial forensic evidence.

programme.title: Ukrainian Sniper
programme.desc: A Ukrainian sniper navigates a warzone and a pregnancy, carving out a place for more women on the frontlines.

programme.title: Blocking The Sun
programme.desc: Wildfires and heat waves are pushing governments to consider a controversial question: Could we artificially reflect the sun from our atmosphere and cool our planet's rising temperature? And more importantly, should we? "In Real Life" rides along in the world's largest flying laboratory and sees the dire stakes surrounding a drastic step to curb climate change.

programme.title: The Keyes To Murder
programme.desc: A self-taught serial killer crisscrosses the country at random, targeting victims of opportunity and murdering them with pre-stashed weapons and tools he calls "kill kits." Law enforcement is clueless to the madman's actions until he breaks his own rule and targets a victim close to home.

programme.title: If The Walls Could Talk
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance and presumed death of a surgeon's wife sparks a feverish homicide investigation, which quickly goes cold. It takes law enforcement 15 years before they can gather enough circumstantial evidence to bring her killer to justice, even though they never find her body.

programme.title: Homicide In The High Desert
programme.desc: When the soon-to-be ex-wife of a prominent investment banker dies inside her Arizona home, it initially appears as though she suffered an accidental fall. Yet, through the dogged persistence of family and friends, and after months of intense investigation, law enforcement discovers the murder was no accident, but an act of rage carried out by a desperate man with hundreds of thousands to gain.

programme.title: Bonded In Murder
programme.desc: A street preacher and his teenage protege run an Internet scam to rob unsuspecting men, steal their identities, then dump their dead bodies in the woods. The scheme works multiple times until one of the marks escapes and brings law enforcement right to their doorstep.

programme.title: Of Vengeance & Murder
programme.desc: A destitute criminal terrorizes central Florida, robbing middles aged men, executing them and disposing of their dead bodies in random wooded areas. The homicides continue for two years until a bloody handprint leads to the capture of a serial killer who happens to be a woman.

programme.title: The Honeybee Killer
programme.desc: A mentally unstable man goes on a two-hour shooting spree, killing one man and wounding two others. Cops have no suspects in that homicide, until the shooter resurfaces to rob a tanning salon and ends up face-to-face with a iron-willed customer.

programme.title: The Bad Son
programme.desc: A gunman attempts to take out an entire family one by one as they enter their house. It takes law enforcement nearly two years to crack the code behind the murders only to find out they were masterminded by someone too close for comfort.

programme.title: Killer Rabbi
programme.desc: When a rabbi's wife is savagely murdered inside the family's suburban New Jersey home, it looks like a robbery gone wrong. After years of chasing down leads, police catch a break, in the form of a co-conspirator whose confession shines a spotlight on a sadistic murder plot.
